breakdown: Naming: Descriptive function/test names like test_process_action_stream_infers_failure_without_explicit_success. Variables like commands, missing_modules, installed_modules clearly indicate purpose. Some generic names like fake_run acceptable for test helpers. Error Handling: Tests properly verify error responses (response[error] == internal error). subprocess.CalledProcessError is raised and caught for fallback package testing. Error conditions explicitly tested. Type Safety: Type hints present throughout (Path, list[list[str]], tuple[list[str], dict[str, object]]). No any types visible. Some mock places use object() without specifics but not critical paths. Architecture: Tests well-organized in separate concerns (stream handling, runtime bootstrap, signature guard, loc script). TestContext and _StreamingContractHarness appropriately encapsulate test harness logic. Module boundaries respected. Test Coverage: Comprehensive tests covering stream failure inference, numpy downgrade skipping, cache invalidation, fallback package candidates, evidence signature guard, loc script with spaces/boundary detection. Assertions verify actual behavior. No empty stubs. Documentation: Descriptive test names explain WHAT is being tested. Comments explain WHY (# noqa: S603 - executes the checked-in repo script under test). _write_file has docstring. No commented-out code.
key_changes: Added test for process_action stream failure inference without explicit success flag
Added test for runtime bootstrap skipping numpy downgrade when repair not needed
Added test for runtime bootstrap cache invalidation and timeout usage
Added test for runtime bootstrap fallback package candidates (onnxruntime-silicon -> onnxruntime)
Added test for evidence signature guard skipping external server runs
Added new test file tests/scripts/test_loc_script.py for loc.sh with spaces and boundary-aware test detection
